acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] IPC mutation success responses include DurabilityReceipt in JSON mode.
- [ ] Durability timeouts return ErrorPayload with retryable=true and receipt populated.
- [ ] ErrorPayload schema matches REALTIME_ERRORS.md (code/message/retryable/details/receipt).
- [ ] API JSON types expose receipts without lossy view structs.
description:
**Problem**
Phase 4: update IPC response shapes for receipts and error payloads. Current `src/daemon/ipc.rs` does not return DurabilityReceipt, and ErrorPayload does not match REALTIME_ERRORS.md.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.14 (IPC protocol evolution), §16.1 (response fields and error payload), §16.3 (CLI flags)

**Files:** src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/api/mod.rs, src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/commands/*, REALTIME_ERRORS.md

**Design**
- Add DurabilityReceipt to success responses and to timeout errors when waiting for replicated durability.
- Replace ErrorPayload shape to `{ code, message, retryable, retry_after_ms?, details?, receipt? }` and keep error codes aligned with REALTIME_ERRORS.md.
- Ensure API JSON types expose receipts without lossy view structs.
- Request field additions and CLI flag plumbing live in bd-3m5.51.
design:
**Design**
- Add optional `namespace`, `durability`, and `client_request_id` fields to mutation requests with defaults (namespace=core, durability=LocalFsync).
- Add DurabilityReceipt to success responses and to timeout errors when waiting for replicated durability.
- Replace ErrorPayload shape to `{ code, message, retryable, retry_after_ms?, details?, receipt? }` and keep error codes aligned with REALTIME_ERRORS.md.
- Plumb CLI flags `--namespace`, `--durability`, `--client-request-id` to IPC requests.
title: Phase 4: IPC requests + receipts (namespace, durability, client_request_id)
Detect force-push and surface explicit warning in sync status

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Lost push races do not erase legacy source metadata or warnings from the returned migrate outcome.
- [ ] Already-canonical repos without origin report a no-remote disposition when the caller did not pass `--no-push`.
- [ ] Regression tests cover retry metadata preservation and the no-origin/no-op outcome case.
description:
**Problem**
Adversarial review found two outcome-contract problems. First, after a retryable push race, the returned migrate outcome can be recomputed from the eventual remote winner instead of the legacy source we actually tried to migrate, which sanitizes `from_effective_version`, deps format, converted-deps, and warnings. Second, an already-canonical repo with no origin currently returns `push: skipped_no_push` and `commit_oid: null`, which makes the rollout docs indistinguishable from a converged fleet-success case even though there was no remote to cut over.

**Files**: crates/beads-git/src/sync.rs, crates/beads-rs/src/cli/backend.rs, crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs, MIGRATION.md
design:
**Design**
Capture the migration source summary before the first rewrite attempt and preserve it across retry loops, even if a later refetch converges on another replica's winner. Returned outcome metadata should describe the source that triggered migration, not whichever canonical ref happened to win the race.

Also distinguish no-origin/no-op from user-requested no-push: when the caller asked for a normal migrate but no origin exists, return a no-remote disposition even for the already-canonical no-op path. Update tests and docs to match.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Audit on 2026-03-11 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover @ 7b5a0b18: migrate outcomes now stay truthful across retry races and no-remote no-op cases. The executor captures the legacy-source summary before the first rewrite attempt and reuses it after a lost push race, so `from_effective_version`, deps format, converted-deps, and warning context are no longer sanitized by the eventual remote winner. The no-op path also distinguishes caller-intent correctly: when no origin-backed store exists and the caller did not ask for `--no-push`, the outcome now reports `push: skipped_no_remote` instead of looking like a converged fleet-success no-op. Coverage includes the lost-race metadata path and the already-canonical no-origin/no-remote no-op case. Verification: `cargo test -p beads-git migrate_store_ref_to_v1 -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p beads-rs migration -- --nocapture`, `cargo test`, `just dylint`, and `cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings` all pass.

title: Migration follow-up: keep outcome metadata truthful across retries and no-remote no-op cases

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Integration tests are grouped by feature.
- [ ] `cargo test` passes with the same coverage.
- [ ] No change to fixture behavior.
description:
**Problem**
- Integration tests are phase-based (`tests/phase*`) which makes it hard to find feature coverage.
- New contributors must know implementation phases to find tests.

**Files:**
- tests/phase*.rs
- tests/fixtures/*
design:
- Rename/move phase tests into feature-based files (`tests/wal_*`, `tests/repl_*`, `tests/checkpoint_*`, `tests/admin_*`, etc).
- Keep test contents unchanged; only file names/organization change.
- Update any references in docs if needed.
title: Reorganize integration tests by feature

description:
**Problem**
Gascity's doctor check runs \`bd config get --json types.custom\` on every \`gc doctor\` invocation (gascity/internal/doctor/checks_custom_types.go:152) and calls \`bd config set types.custom <value>\` to register custom types (same file line 179). beads-rs has NO \`bd config\` command at all — gascity gets an unrecognized-subcommand error.

Reference: docs/go-parity/cmds/gascity/02-config.md.

**Design**
Add \`bd config\` subcommand family:

- \`bd config set <key> <value>\` — write a single config entry
- \`bd config get <key>\` — read a single config entry (human output)
- \`bd config get --json <key>\` — machine output, matches Go's shape
- \`bd config list\` — enumerate keys
- \`bd config unset <key>\` — delete a key

**Where config lives:**

Config values become part of the store's replicable state (in the \`core\` namespace), OR in a per-repo config file (\`.beads/config.toml\`), OR in a dedicated namespace. Decide based on whether config should sync across replicas:

- \`types.custom\` — MUST sync (otherwise \`bd create -t molecule\` works on one replica but errors on another)
- \`types.aliases\` — SHOULD sync
- Local-only config (e.g., per-replica auth, cache paths) — MUST NOT sync

Recommendation: split. Replicable config (types.custom, types.aliases, workflow defaults) goes in a dedicated \`config\` namespace with \`replicate_mode = everywhere\`; per-repo local settings go in a file.

For Floor 1, scope to just the gascity-required keys (types.custom, types.aliases). Broader config architecture can be its own later design.

**Required shape compat:** \`bd config get --json types.custom\` must return a shape gascity can parse (see cmds/gascity/02-config.md for the Go shape).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] \`bd config set types.custom molecule,wisp,convoy,gate\` works
- [ ] \`bd config get --json types.custom\` returns the shape Go emits
- [ ] \`bd config set\` + \`bd config get\` round-trip across namespace sync (two replicas see the same config after sync)
- [ ] \`bd config list\` enumerates set keys
- [ ] \`bd config unset <key>\` removes an entry
- [ ] Gascity's \`doctor/checks_custom_types.go\` flow passes end-to-end
- [ ] \`cargo xtest\` green

**Independent of**
Floor 1 work except F1.A (BeadType open enum) — if F1.A uses config-driven custom types, this bead lands first or in lockstep. They're mutually reinforcing but each is useful without the other (F1.A can start with a compiled-in list; F1.H can start with a separate config store).

**Files likely touched**
- \`crates/beads-cli/src/commands/\` (new \`config.rs\`)
- \`crates/beads-core/\` (config storage primitive — probably a new typed set of keys)
- Replication/sync tests that exercise config round-trip
title: Floor 1 CLI: bd config set/get subcommand family
